stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • In 2017, paleontology professor Steve Brusatte and his students from the University of Edinburgh in Scotland made a discovery.
  • The team found a partial dinosaur skeleton at a site on Scotland's Isle of Skye.
  • The skeleton belonged to a dinosaur species that lived approximately 166 million years ago.
  • The new species was named Dearc sgiathanach to honor the location where it was found, using a Scottish Gaelic phrase meaning "winged reptile from Skye."
stem: The student wants to explain the origin of the species' name. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Dearc sgiathanach, a new species discovered in Scotland in 2017, lived approximately 166 million years ago.
A partial dinosaur skeleton found on Scotland's Isle of Skye belonged to a species named Dearc sgiathanach.
The new species was named Dearc sgiathanach, a Scottish Gaelic phrase meaning "winged reptile from Skye," to honor the location where the fossil was found.
Dearc sgiathanach, a species that lived approximately 166 million years ago, was discovered in 2017 by Steve Brusatte and a team of university students.

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. It explains where the dinosaur's name came from.

Choice A is incorrect. This choice does not explain the origin of the dinosaur's name. Choice B is incorrect. This choice does not explain the origin of the dinosaur's name. Choice D is incorrect. This choice does not explain the origin of the dinosaur's name.
